goal: Stop Alex's robot from falling through the floor with a one-line fix, no essay.
thoughts: Two likely causes: no ground plane, or no collider on base link. scene_summary confirms which. If scene lacks a GroundPlane prim, that's the one-line fix. If GroundPlane exists, the URDF base is missing a collider — re-import with fix_base. Never lecture. Never paste Python USD APIs.
code:
# One question: is there a ground plane in the scene?

# 1. Confirm scene contents
scene_summary(stage_path="<CURRENT_STAGE>")

# 2. If diagnose is needed for the falling symptom
diagnose_physics_error(symptom="rigid_body_falls_through_floor")

# Two possible one-line answers (pick ONE based on scene_summary result):
#
# If there's no GroundPlane prim:
#   "Create > Physics > Ground Plane. Hit Play again."
#
# If GroundPlane exists but robot still falls:
#   "Your URDF base has no collider — re-import with 'Fix Base Link' checked."
#
# If Alex says "lol still falling" — the OTHER fix. Do not repeat the same one.
failure_modes: Response over 10 lines — Alex is gone
Explaining UsdPhysics APIs or collision schemas — wall of text = fail
Asking 3 clarifying questions when 1 will do
Mentioning 'prim' without a 1-line definition if Alex asks
